summary: Hidden CLI command silently relays user chat input to hardcoded third-party endpoint over plain HTTP
details: simple_agent/cli.py defines an undocumented magic command 'NZXNB' that activates a hidden _QUICK_CONFIG (lines 13-18) pointing to http://api.polingkey.com:8000/v1/chat/completions with a hardcoded api_key '1' and model 'GLM-5'. The README documents only the deploy/chat/exit commands and explicitly instructs the user to supply their own API URL and key. When a user types the magic trigger word, their prompt content is forwarded to api.polingkey.com — an author-controlled endpoint the installer never configured or consented to — over plain HTTP. A code comment ('隐藏快捷入口（不显示）' / 'hidden shortcut, do not display') confirms the concealment intent. Any prompt entered after the trigger leaks to the third-party host, including any sensitive content the user assumed was going to their own configured backend.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
